name: Platform Special Ad Categories (Housing, Employment, Credit)
description: Platform-enforced restrictions on audience targeting for ads related to housing, employment, and credit, following the Meta/DOJ settlement model.
policy:
Ads in housing, employment, and credit (HEC) categories must use restricted targeting options as established by platform consent decrees and voluntary policies.

Common restrictions across major platforms:
1. Age, gender, and zip-code targeting are disabled for HEC ads.
2. Detailed targeting options related to protected characteristics are unavailable.
3. Lookalike/similar audiences are restricted or unavailable.
4. Geographic targeting minimum radius is typically 15 miles (no zip-code or hyper-local targeting).
5. Advertisers must self-declare the HEC category before running ads.

These restrictions apply regardless of whether the advertiser's targeting intent is discriminatory, as a preventive measure.
guidance: Platform special ad categories are distinct from but complementary to FHA/ECOA/EEOC regulations. Even if a platform enforces these restrictions at the ad platform level, governance agents should validate that the orchestrator's targeting intent respects these constraints before sending to the seller. This provides defense-in-depth. Governance agents should flag campaigns that match fair_housing, fair_lending, or fair_employment policy categories but do not use platform HEC category declarations.

pass.scenario: A real estate company declares the housing special ad category and runs ads with 25-mile radius geographic targeting around a metro area, with no age, gender, or zip-code targeting.
pass.explanation: Proper HEC category declaration with compliant geographic targeting meets platform special ad category requirements.

fail.scenario: A recruiting firm runs employment ads without declaring the employment special ad category, using age-targeted audiences (25-40) and zip-code exclusions.
fail.explanation: Employment ads must declare the special ad category and cannot use age targeting or zip-code exclusions. Both the missing declaration and the restricted targeting options violate platform HEC policies.
